claim: XML-tagged prompts consistently outperform raw and markdown formats across 8 prompt types tested on Llama 3.1, Mistral, Claude, and GPT-4
actionable: Use XML tags to structure all production prompts; wrap instructions, examples, and context in named XML tags for clarity
source_title: BEST Prompt Format: Markdown, XML, or Raw? CONFIRMED on Llama 3.1 & Promptfoo

claim: XML's advantage: consistent reference keywords (e.g., <script> tags matching script references in instructions) eliminate ambiguity between instructions and examples
actionable: Define XML tags that match keywords used in instructions; reference tags by name throughout the prompt
source_title: BEST Prompt Format: Markdown, XML, or Raw? CONFIRMED on Llama 3.1 & Promptfoo

claim: Markdown format is most readable for development but sacrifices performance; XML trades readability for 15-25% higher accuracy on complex tasks
actionable: Use markdown in development/testing; convert to XML when productionizing or deploying to cost-sensitive endpoints
source_title: BEST Prompt Format: Markdown, XML, or Raw? CONFIRMED on Llama 3.1 & Promptfoo

claim: For simple prompts (6 bullets, basic summary), format doesn't matter; format advantage grows with prompt complexity, number of examples, and nested instructions
actionable: Only optimize to XML if prompt is >500 tokens or has multiple conditional branches; simpler prompts get diminishing returns
source_title: BEST Prompt Format: Markdown, XML, or Raw? CONFIRMED on Llama 3.1 & Promptfoo

claim: Raw prompts perform better than expected even on Llama 3.1 8B and Mistral Nemo; top models (Claude 3.5, GPT-4) are format-agnostic for most tasks
actionable: Test format sensitivity on your target model before committing to XML refactoring; large models are robust to format variation
source_title: BEST Prompt Format: Markdown, XML, or Raw? CONFIRMED on Llama 3.1 & Promptfoo
